author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter II. 23–25; III. 1–5.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Opportunely has the Lord procured for us that this passage should occur in its order to day: for I suppose you have observed, beloved, that we have undertaken to consider and explain the Gospel according to John in due course.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Opportunely then it occurs, that to-day you should hear from the Gospel, that, “Except a man be born again of water and of the Spirit, he shall not see the kingdom of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is time that we exhort you, who are still catechumens, who have believed in Christ in such wise, that you are still bearing your sins.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And none shall see the kingdom of heaven while burdened with sins; for none shall reign with Christ, but he to whom they have been forgiven: but forgiven they cannot be, but to him who is born again of water and of the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But let us observe all the words what they imply, that here the sluggish may find with what earnestness they must haste to put off their burden.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For were they bearing some heavy load, either of stone, or of wood, or even of some gain; if they were carrying corn, or wine, or money, they would run to put off their loads: they are carrying a burden of sins, and yet are sluggish to run.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You must run to put off this burden; it weighs you down, it drowns you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, you have heard that when our Lord Jesus Christ “was in Jerusalem at the Passover, on the feast day, many believed in His name, seeing the signs which He did.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Many believed in His name;” and what follows?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But Jesus did not trust Himself to them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now what does this mean, “They believed,” or trusted, “in His name;” and yet “Jesus did not trust Himself to them;”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it, perhaps, that they had not believed on Him, but were feigning to have believed, and that therefore Jesus did not trust Himself to them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the evangelist would not have said, “Many believed in His name,” if he were not giving a true testimony to them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A great thing, then, it is, and a wonderful thing: men believe on Christ, and Christ trusts not Himself to men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Especially is it wonderful, since, being the Son of God, He of course suffered willingly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If He were not willing, He would never have suffered, since, had He not willed it, He had not been born; and if He had willed this only, merely to be born and not to die, He might have done even whatever He willed, because He is the almighty Son of the almighty Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us prove it by facts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when they wished to hold Him, He departed from them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Gospel says, “And when they would have cast Him headlong from the top of the mountain, He departed from them unhurt.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when they came to lay hold of Him, after He was sold by Judas the traitor, who imagined that he had it in his power to deliver up his Master and Lord, there also the Lord showed that He suffered of His own will, not of necessity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when the Jews desired to lay hold of Him, He said to them, “Whom seek ye?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But they said, Jesus of Nazareth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And said He, I am He.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: On hearing this saying, they went backward, and fell to the ground.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this, that in answering them He threw them to the ground, He showed His power; that in His being taken by them He might show His will.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was of compassion, then, that He suffered.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “He was delivered up for our sins, and rose again for our justification.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear His own words: “I have power to lay down my life, and I have power to take it again: no man taketh it from me, but I lay it down of myself, that I may take it again.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since, therefore, He had such power, since He declared it by words, showed it by deeds, what then does it mean that Jesus did not trust Himself to them, as if they would do Him some harm against His will, or would do something to Him against His will, especially seeing that they had already believed in His name?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, of the same persons the evangelist says, “They believed in His name,” of whom he says, “But Jesus did not trust Himself to them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because He knew all men, and needed not that any should bear witness of man: for Himself knew what was in man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The artificer knew what was in His own work better than the work knew what was in itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Creator of man knew what was in man, which the created man himself knew not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do we not prove this of Peter, that he knew not what was in himself, when he said, “With Thee, even to death”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear that the Lord knew what was in man: “Thou with me even to death?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The man, then, knew not what was in himself; but the Creator of the man knew what was in the man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless, many believed in His name, and yet Jesus did not trust Himself to them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What can we say, brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps the circumstances that follow will indicate to us what the mystery of these words is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That men had believed in Him is manifest, is true; none doubts it, the Gospel says it, the truth-speaking evangelist testifies to it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Again, that Jesus trusted not Himself to them is also manifest, and no Christian doubts it; for the Gospel says this also, and the same truth-speaking evangelist testifies to it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, then, is it that they believed in His name, and yet Jesus did not trust Himself to them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us see what follows.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And there was a man of the Pharisees, Nicodemus by name, a ruler of the Jews: the same came to Him by night, and said unto Him, Rabbi (you already know that Master is called Rabbi), we know that Thou art a teacher come from God; for no man can do these signs which Thou doest, except God be with him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This Nicodemus, then, was of those who had believed in His name, as they saw the signs and prodigies which He did.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this is what he said above: “Now, when He was in Jerusalem at the passover on the feast-day, many believed in His name.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why did they believe?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He goes on to say, “Seeing His signs which He did.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what says he of Nicodemus?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “There was a ruler of the Jews, Nicodemus by name the same came to Him by night, and says to Him, Rabbi, we know that Thou art a teacher come from God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore this man also had believed in His name.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And why had he believed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He goes on, “For no man can do these signs which Thou doest, except God be with him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, therefore, Nicodemus was of those who had believed in His name, let us now consider, in the case of this Nicodemus, why Jesus did not trust Himself to them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus answered and said unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore to them who have been born again doth Jesus trust Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, those men had believed on Him, and yet Jesus trusted not Himself to them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such are all catechumens: already they believe in the name of Christ, but Jesus does not trust Himself to them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give good heed, my beloved, and understand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we say to a catechumen, Dost thou believe on Christ, he answers, I believe, and signs himself; already he bears the cross of Christ on his forehead, and is not ashamed of the cross of his Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, he has believed in His name.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us ask him, Dost thou eat the flesh of the Son of man, and drink the blood of the Son of man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He knows not what we say, because Jesus has not trusted Himself to him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, since Nicodemus was of that number, he came to the Lord, but came by night; and this perhaps pertains to the matter.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Came to the Lord, and came by night; came to the Light, and came in the darkness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what do they that are born again of water and of the Spirit hear from the apostle?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Ye were once darkness, but now light in the Lord; walk as children of light;” and again, “But we who are of the day, let us be sober.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore they who are born again were of the night, and are of the day; were darkness, and are light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now Jesus trusts Himself to them, and they come to Jesus, not by night, like Nicodemus; not in darkness do they seek the day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For such now also profess: Jesus has come near to them, has made salvation in them; for He said, “Except a man eat my flesh, and drink my blood, he shall not have life in him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And as the catechumens have the sign of the cross on their forehead, they are already of the great house; but from servants let them become sons.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they are something who already belong to the great house.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when did the people Israel eat the manna?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: After they had passed the Red Sea.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And as to what the Red Sea signifies, hear the apostle: “Moreover, brethren, I would not have you ignorant, that all our fathers were under the cloud, and all passed through the sea.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To what purpose passed they through the sea?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As if thou wert asking of him, he goes on to say, “And all were baptized by Moses in the cloud and in the sea.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now, if the figure of the sea had such efficacy, how great will be the efficacy of the true form of baptism!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If what was done in a figure brought the people, after they had crossed over, to the manna, what will Christ impart, in the verity of His baptism, to His own people, brought over through Himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By His baptism He brings over them that believe; all their sins, the enemies as it were that pursue them, being slain, as all the Egyptians perished in that sea.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whither does He bring over, my brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whither does Jesus bring over by baptism, of which Moses then showed the figure, when he brought them through the sea?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the manna.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is the manna?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I am,” saith He, “the living bread, which came down from heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The faithful receive the manna, having now been brought through the Red Sea?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why Red Sea?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Besides sea, why also “red”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That “Red Sea” signified the baptism of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How is the baptism of Christ red, but as consecrated by Christ’s blood?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whither, then, does He lead those that believe and are baptized?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the manna.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, “manna,” I say: what the Jews, that people Israel, received, is well known, well known what God had rained on them from heaven; and yet catechumens know not what Christians receive.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let them blush, then, for their ignorance; let them pass through the Red Sea, let them eat the manna, that as they have believed in the name of Jesus, so likewise Jesus may trust Himself to them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore mark, my brethren, what answer this man who came to Jesus by night makes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Although he came to Jesus, yet because he came by night, he still speaks from the darkness of his own flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He understands not what he hears from the Lord, understands not what he hears from the Light, “which lighteth every man that cometh into this world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Already hath the Lord said to him, “Except a man be born again, he shall not see the kingdom of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nicodemus saith unto Him, How can a man be born again when he is old?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Spirit speaks to him, and he thinks of the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He thinks of his own flesh, because as yet he thinks not of Christ’s flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when the Lord Jesus had said, “Except a man eat my flesh, and drink my blood, he shall not have life in him,” some who followed Him were offended, and said among themselves, “This is a hard saying; who can hear it?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they fancied that, in saying this, Jesus meant that they would be able to cook Him, after being cut up like a lamb, and eat Him: horrified at His words, they went back, and no more followed Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus speaks the evangelist: “And the Lord Himself remained with the twelve; and they said to Him, Lo, those have left Thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He said, Will ye also go away?”—wishing to show them that He was necessary to them, not they necessary to Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let no man fancy that he frightens Christ, when he tells Him that he is a Christian; as if Christ will be more blessed if thou be a Christian.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is a good thing for thee to be a Christian; but if thou be not, it will not be ill for Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear the voice of the psalm, “I said to the Lord, Thou art my God, since Thou hast no need of my goods.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For that reason, “Thou art my God, since of my goods Thou hast no need.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou be without God, thou wilt be less; if thou be with God, God will not be greater.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not from thee will He be greater, but thou without Him wilt be less.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Grow, therefore, in Him; do not withdraw thyself, that He may, as it were, diminish.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou wilt be renewed if thou come to Him, wilt suffer loss if thou depart from Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He remains entire when thou comest to Him, remains entire even when thou fallest away.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When, therefore, He had said to His disciples, “Will ye also go away?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Peter, that Rock, answered with the voice of all, “Lord, to whom shall we go?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast the words of eternal life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Pleasantly savored the Lord’s flesh in his mouth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord, however, expounded to them, and said, “It is the Spirit that quickeneth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: After He had said, “Except a man eat my flesh, and drink my blood, he shall not have life in him,” lest they should understand it carnally, He said, “It is the Spirit that quickeneth, but the flesh profiteth nothing: the words which I have spoken unto you are spirit and life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This Nicodemus, who had come to Jesus by night, did not savor of this spirit and this life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Saith Jesus to him, “Except a man be born again, he shall not see the kingdom of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And he, savoring of his own flesh, while as yet he savored not of the flesh of Christ in his mouth, saith, “How can a man be born a second time, when he is old?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Can he enter a second time into his mother’s womb, and be born?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This man knew but one birth, that from Adam and Eve; that which is from God and the Church he knew not yet: he knew only those parents that bring forth to death, knew not yet the parents that bring forth to life; he knew but the parents that bring forth successors, knew not yet the ever-living parents that bring forth those that shall abide.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whilst there are two births, then, he understood only one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: One is of the earth, the other of heaven; one of the flesh, the other of the Spirit; one of mortality, the other of eternity; one of male and female, the other of God and the Church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But these two are each single; there can be no repeating the one or the other.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rightly did Nicodemus understand the birth of the flesh; so understand thou also the birth of the Spirit, as Nicodemus understood the birth of the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What did Nicodemus understand?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Can a man enter a second time into his mother’s womb, and be born?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus, whosoever shall tell thee to be spiritually born a second time, answer in the words of Nicodemus, “Can a man enter a second time into his mother’s womb, and be born?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am already born of Adam, Adam cannot beget me a second time.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am already born of Christ, Christ cannot beget me again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As there is no repeating from the womb, so neither from baptism.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that is born of the Catholic Church, is born, as it were, of Sarah, of the free woman; he that is born of heresy is, as it were, born of the bond woman, but of Abraham’s seed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consider, beloved, how great a mystery.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God testifies, saying, “I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Were there not other patriarchs?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Before these, was there not holy Noah, who alone of the whole human race, with all his house, was worthy to be delivered from the flood,—he in whom, and in his sons, the Church was prefigured?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Borne by wood, they escaped the flood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then afterwards great men whom we know, whom Holy Scriptures commends, Moses faithful in all his house.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet those three are named, just as if they alone deserved well of him: “I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob: this is my name for ever.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Sublime mystery!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is the Lord that is able to open both our mouth and your hearts, that we may speak as He has deigned to reveal, and that you may receive even as it is expedient for you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The patriarchs, then, are these three, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You know that the sons of Jacob were twelve, and thence the people Israel; for Jacob himself is Israel, and the people Israel in twelve tribes pertaining to the twelve sons of Israel.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob three fathers, and one people.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The fathers three, as it were in the beginning of the people; three fathers in whom the people was figured: and the former people itself the present people.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in the Jewish people was figured the Christian people.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There a figure, here the truth; there a shadow, here the body: as the apostle says, “Now these things happened to them in a figure.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is the apostle’s voice: “They were written,” saith he, “for our sakes, upon whom the end of the ages is come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let your mind now recur to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the case of these three, we find that free women bear children, and that bond women bear children: we find there offspring of free women, we find there also offspring of bond women.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The bond woman signifies nothing good: “Cast out the bond woman,” saith he, “and her son; for the son of the bond woman shall not be heir with the son of the free.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The apostle recounts this; and he says that in those two sons of Abraham was a figure of the two Testaments, the Old and the New.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the Old Testament belong the lovers of temporal things, the lovers of the world: to the New Testament belong the lovers of eternal life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence, that Jerusalem on earth was the shadow of the heavenly Jerusalem, the mother of us all, which is in heaven; and these are the apostle’s words.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And of that city from which we are absent on our sojourn, you know much, you have now heard much.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But we find a wonderful thing in these births, in these fruits of the womb, in these generations of free and bond women: namely, four sorts of men; in which four sorts is completed the figure of the future Christian people, so that what was said in the case of those three patriarchs is not surprising, “I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in the case of all Christians, observe, brethren, either good men are born of evil men, or evil men of good; or good men of good, or evil men of evil: more than these four sorts you cannot find.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These things I will again repeat: Give heed, keep them, excite your hearts, be not dull; take in, lest ye be taken, how of all Christians there are four sorts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Either of the good are born good, or of the evil, are born evil; or of the good are born evil, or of the evil good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I think it is plain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of the good, good; if they who baptize are good, and also they who are baptized rightly believe, and are rightly numbered among the members of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of the evil, evil; if they who baptize are evil, and they who are baptized approach God with a double heart, and do not observe the morals which they hear urged in the Church, so as not to be chaff, but grain, there.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How many such there are, you know, beloved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of the evil, good; sometimes an adulterer baptizes, and he that is baptized is justified.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of the good, evil; sometimes they who baptize are holy, they who are baptized do not desire to keep the way of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I suppose, brethren, that this is known in the Church, and that what we are saying is manifest by daily examples; but let us consider these things in the case of our fathers before us, how they also had these four kinds.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of the good, good; Ananias baptized Paul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How of the evil, evil?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The apostle declares that there were certain preachers of the gospel, who, he says, did not use to preach the gospel with a pure motive, whom, however, he tolerates in the Christian society, saying, “What then, notwithstanding every way, whether by occasion or in truth, Christ is preached, and in this I rejoice.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was he therefore malevolent, and did he rejoice in another’s evil?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No, but rejoiced because through evil men the truth was preached, and by the mouths of evil men Christ was preached.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If these men baptized any persons like themselves, evil men baptized evil men: if they baptized such as the Lord admonishes, when He says, “Whatsoever they bid you, do; but do not ye after their works,” they were evil men that were baptizing good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Good men baptized evil men, as Simon the sorcerer was baptized by Philip, a holy man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore these four sorts, my brethren, are known.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, I repeat them again, hold them, count them, think upon them; guard against what is evil; keep what is good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Good men are born of good, when holy men are baptized by holy; evil men are born of evil, when both they that baptize and they that are baptized live unrighteously and ungodly; good men are born of evil, when they are evil that baptize, and they good that are baptized; evil men are born of good, when they are good that baptize, and they evil that are baptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How do we find this in these three names, “I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We hold the bond women among the evil, and the free women among the good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Free women bear the good; Sarah bare Isaac: bond women bear the evil; Hagar bare Ishmael.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have in the case of Abraham alone the two sorts, both when the good are of the good, and also when the evil are of the evil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But where have we evil of good figured?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rebecca, Isaac’s wife, was a free woman: read, She bare twins; one was good, the other evil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast the Scripture openly declaring by the voice of God, “Jacob have I loved, but Esau have I hated.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rebecca bare those two, Jacob and Esau: one of them is chosen, the other is reprobated; one succeeds to the inheritance, the other is disinherited.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God does not make His people of Esau, but makes it of Jacob.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The seed is one, those conceived are dissimilar: the womb is one, those born of it are diverse.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was not the free woman that bare Jacob, the same free woman that bare Esau?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They strove in the mother’s womb; and when they strove there, it was said to Rebecca, “Two peoples are in thy womb.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Two men, two peoples; a good people, and a bad people: but yet they strive in one womb.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How many evil men there are in the Church!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And one womb carries them until they are separated in the end: and the good cry out against the evil, and the evil in turn cry out against the good, and both strive together in the bowels of one mother.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Will they be always together?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is a going forth to the light in the end; the birth which is here figured in a mystery is declared; and it will then appear that “Jacob have I loved, but Esau have I hated.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly we have now found, brethren, of the good, good—of the free woman, Isaac; and of the evil, evil—of the bond woman, Ishmael; and of the good, evil—of Rebecca, Esau: where shall we find of the evil, good?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There remains Jacob, that the completion of these four sorts may be concluded in the three patriarchs.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jacob had for wives free women, he had also bond women: the free bear children, as do also the bond, and thus come the twelve sons of Israel.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If you count them all, of whom they were born, they were not all of the free women, nor all of the bond women; but yet they were all of one seed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, my brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did not they who were born of the bond women possess the land of promise together with their brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have there found good sons of Jacob born of bond women, and good sons of Jacob born of free women.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Their birth of the wombs of bond women was nothing against them, when they knew their seed in the father, and consequently they held the kingdom with their brethren.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, as in the case of Jacob’s sons, that they were born of bond women did not hinder their holding the kingdom, and receiving the land of promise on an equality with their brothers; their birth of bond women did not hinder them, but the father’s seed prevailed: so, whoever are baptized by evil men, appear as if born of bond women; nevertheless, because they are of the seed of the Word of God, which is figured in Jacob, let them not be cast down, they shall possess the inheritance with their brethren.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, let him who is born of the good seed be without fear; only let him not imitate the bond woman, if he is born of a bond woman.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not thou imitate the evil, proud, bond woman.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how came the sons of Jacob, that were born of bond women, to possess the land of promise with their brethren, whilst Ishmael, born of a bond woman, was cast out from the inheritance?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, but because he was proud, they were humble?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He proudly reared his neck, and wished to seduce his brother while he was playing with him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A great mystery is there.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They were playing together, Ishmael and Isaac: Sarah sees them playing, and says to Abraham, “Cast out the bond woman and her son; for the son of the bond woman shall not be heir with my son Isaac.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when Abraham was sorrowful, the Lord confirmed to him the saying of his wife.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now here is evidently a mystery, that the event was somehow pregnant with something future.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She sees them playing, and says, “Cast out the bond woman and her son.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this, brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what evil had Ishmael done to the boy Isaac, in playing with him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That playing was a mocking; that playing signified deception.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now attend, beloved, to this great mystery.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The apostle calls it persecution; that playing, that play, he calls persecution: for he says, “But as then he that was born after the flesh, persecuted him that was born after the Spirit, so also now;” that is, they that are born after the flesh persecute them that are born after the Spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who are born after the flesh?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lovers of the world, lovers of this life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who are born after the Spirit?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lovers of the kingdom of heaven, lovers of Christ, men that long for eternal life, that worship God freely.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They play, and the apostle calls it persecution.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For after he said these words, “And as then he that was born after the flesh persecuted him that was born after the Spirit, so also now;” the apostle went on, and showed of what persecution, he was speaking: “But what says the Scripture?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Cast out the bond woman and her son: for the son of the bond woman shall not be heir with my son Isaac.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We search where the Scripture says this, to see whether any persecution on Ishmael’s part against Isaac preceded this; and we find that this was said by Sarah when she saw the boys playing together.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The playing which Scripture says that Sarah saw, the apostle calls persecution.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence, they who seduce you by playing, persecute you the more.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Come,” say they, “Come, be baptized here, here is true baptism for thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not play, there is one true baptism; that other is play: thou wilt be seduced, and that will be a grievous persecution to thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It were better for thee to make Ishmael a present of the kingdom; but Ishmael will not have it, for he means to play.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Keep thou thy father’s inheritance, and hear this: “Cast out the bond woman and her son; for the son of the bond woman shall not be heir with my son Isaac.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These men, too, dare to say that they are wont to suffer persecution from catholic kings, or from catholic princes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What persecution do they bear?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Affliction of body: yet if at times they have suffered, and how they suffered, let themselves know, and settle it with their consciences; still they suffered only affliction of body: the persecution which they cause is more grievous.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Beware when Ishmael wishes to play with Isaac, when he fawns on thee, when he offers another baptism: answer him, I have baptism already.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if this baptism is true, he who would give thee another would be mocking thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Beware of the persecution of the soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For though the party of Donatus has at times suffered somewhat at the hands of catholic princes, it was a bodily suffering, not the suffering of spiritual deception.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear and see in the very facts of Old Testament history all the signs and indications of things to come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Sarah is found to have afflicted her maid Hagar: Sarah is free.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: After her maid began to be proud, Sarah complained to Abraham, and said, “Cast out the bond woman;” she has lifted her neck against me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His wife complains of Abraham, as if it were his doing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But Abraham, who was not bound to the maid by lust, but by the duty of begetting children, inasmuch as Sarah had given her to him to have offspring by her, says to her: “Behold, she is thy handmaid; do unto her as thou wilt.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And Sarah grievously afflicted her, and she fled from her face.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, the free woman afflicted the bond woman, and the apostle does not call that a persecution; the slave plays with his master, and he calls it persecution: this afflicting is not called persecution; that playing is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How does it appear to you, brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do you not understand what is signified?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus, then, when God wills to stir up powers against heretics, against schismatics, against those that scatter the Church, that blow on Christ as if they abhorred Him, that blaspheme baptism, let them not wonder; because God stirs them up, that Hagar may be beaten by Sarah.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let Hagar know herself, and yield her neck: for when, after being humiliated, she departed from her mistress, an angel met her, and said to her, “What is the matter with thee, Hagar, Sarah’s handmaid?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When she complained of her mistress, what did she hear from the angel?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Return to thy mistress.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is for this that she is afflicted, that she may return; and would that she may return, for her offspring, just like the sons of Jacob, will obtain the inheritance with their brethren.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But they wonder that Christian powers are roused against detestable scatterers of the Church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Should they not be moved, then?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How otherwise should they give an account of their rule to God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Observe, beloved, what I say, that it concerns Christian kings of this world to wish their mother the Church, of which they have been spiritually born, to have peace in their times.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We read Daniel’s visions and prophetical histories.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The three children praised the Lord in the fire: King Nebuchadnezzar wondered at the children praising God, and at the fire around them doing them no harm: and whilst he wondered, what did King Nebuchadnezzar say, he who was neither a Jew nor circumcised, who had set up his own image and compelled all men to adore it; but, impressed by the praises of the three children when he saw the majesty of God present in the fire what said he?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And I will publish a decree to all tribes and tongues in the whole earth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What sort of decree?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Whosoever shall speak blasphemy against the God of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, shall be cut off, and their houses shall be made a ruin.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See how an alien king acts with raging indignation that the God of Israel might not be blasphemed, because He was able to deliver the three children from the fire: and yet they would not have Christian kings to act with severity when Christ is contemptuously rejected, by whom not three children, but the whole world, with these very kings, is delivered from the fire of hell!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For those three children, my brethren, were delivered from temporal fire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is He not the same God who was the God of the Maccabees and the God of the three children?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The latter He delivered from the fire; the former did in body perish in the torments of fire, but in mind they remained steadfast in the ordinances of the law.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The latter were openly delivered, the former were crowned in secret.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is a greater thing to be delivered from the flame of hell than from the furnace of a human power.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, Nebuchadnezzar praised and extolled and gave glory to God because He delivered three children from the fire, and gave such glory as to send forth a decree throughout his kingdom, “Whosoever shall speak blasphemy against the God of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, shall be cut off, and their houses shall be brought to ruin,” how should not these kings be moved, who observe, not three children delivered from the flame, but their very selves delivered from hell, when they see Christ, by whom they have been delivered, contemptuously spurned in Christians, when they hear it said to a Christian, “Say that thou art not a Christian”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Men are willing to do such deeds, but they do not wish to suffer, at all events, such punishments.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For see what they do and what they suffer.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They slay souls, they suffer in body: they cause everlasting deaths, and yet they complain that they themselves suffer temporal deaths.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet what deaths do they suffer?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They allege to us some martyrs of theirs in persecution.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, Marculus was hurled headlong from a rock; see, Donatus of Bagaia was thrown into a well.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When have the Roman authorities decreed such punishments as casting men down rocks?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what do those of our party reply?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What was done I know not; what however do ours tell?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That they flung themselves headlong and cast the infamy of it upon the authorities.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us call to mind the custom of the Roman authorities, and see to whom we are to give credit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Our men declare that those men cast themselves down headlong.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If they are not the very disciples of those men, who now cast themselves down precipices, while no man persecutes them, let us not credit the allegation of our men: what wonder if those men did what these are wont to do?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Roman authorities never did employ such punishments: for had they not the power to put them to death openly?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But those men, while they wished to be honored when dead, found not a death to make them more famous.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In short, whatever the fact was, I do not know.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And even if thou hast suffered corporal affliction, O party of Donatus, at the hand of the Catholic Church, as an Hagar thou hast suf fered it at the hand of Sarah; “return to thy mistress.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A point which it was indeed necessary to discuss has detained us somewhat too long to be at all able to expound the whole text of the Gospel Lesson.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let this suffice you in the meantime, beloved brethren, lest, by speaking of other matters, what has been spoken might be shut out from your hearts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hold fast these things, declare such things; and while yourselves are inflamed, go your way thither, and set on fire them that are cold.
